text: Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity using the photovoltaic effect. When sunlight hits a panel, photons dislodge electrons from silicon atoms. An internal electric field forces these loose electrons to flow in a specific direction, creating an electrical current that powers your home. YouTube·The Engineering Mindset +3The process unfolds in four main steps: 1. Light Absorption Solar panels are made of many individual solar cells, typically constructed from silicon, which acts as a semiconductor. When the sun shines on the panel, it delivers particles of energy called photons. The panel absorbs these photons, transferring their energy to the silicon. YouTube·Saibah Instrumentation +12. Electron Movement Silicon in solar cells is chemically treated (or "doped") to create two distinct layers: a negative layer (with extra electrons) and a positive layer (missing electrons). Where these two layers meet, a permanent electric field is created. When photons strike the silicon, they knock electrons free. The electric field then acts as an invisible force, pushing these free electrons in a single, specific direction. Aurora Solar +23. Collecting the Current Thin conductive metal plates and wires in the solar cell capture these moving electrons, turning them into a usable electrical circuit. At this point, the electricity produced flows in only one direction, which is known as Direct Current (DC). Maxeon Solar Technologies +34. Conversion (DC to AC) Because most household appliances and the electrical grid operate on Alternating Current (AC), the electricity cannot be used immediately. It first flows to a device called an inverter. The inverter converts the DC electricity into AC electricity, making it safe to power your electronics. YouTube·The Energy Professor +1For a step-by-step 3D animation breaking down exactly how electrons flow to generate electricity:1mHow Do Solar Panel Work? (3D Animation)3D RequiemYouTube• 9 mag 2024What Happens Next? Powering your home: The AC electricity flows into your home's electrical panel to run your appliances.Energy Storage or Grid Sharing: If your panels generate more electricity than you are currently using, the excess can either be sent to the local power grid (often earning you credits through net metering) or stored in a home battery system. YouTube·The Energy Professor +1To explore the differences between various home solar setups (like grid-tied systems and batteries):58sHow Home Solar Systems Work: A Beginner's GuideSolarQuotesYouTube• 21 lug 2025
